{{Infobox artifact
| name = Eurias
| introduced = lh-01-the-captain
}}

''See also: [[Eurias Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Eurias, also known as the Life-Giver, is a highly potent magical focus shaped like a tall staff made of smooth, pale wood. It is topped with a blue crystal orb that contains the remnants of a [[World Spirit]] from a destroyed ocean planet. As a magical tool, its primary function is amplification rather than precision; it excels at large-scale feats, such as summoning massive rainstorms across entire hemispheres or manipulating vast quantities of water and [[Aether]], but it is less effective for targeting specific individuals. The staff possesses a powerful connection to [[life]] and water, capable of generating sudden growth of vegetation, rivers, and localized weather patterns like wind and rain. It can also be used to power massive sealing spells or to expand a mage's magical influence and reach. Due to its immense energy, the staff can be sensed from great distances and has been used by powerful figures to clash with titans or manipulate planetary elements. While extremely difficult to destroy, the staff requires careful management, as its unsealed power can drain a user's [[Aether]] or cause unintended environmental destruction.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Eurias is a unique, one-of-a-kind magical focus known as the Life-Giver that cannot be easily understood or copied. This powerful staff, which [[Varic Vallenar]] carries and holds as a heavy weight in his mind, consists of a foot-long rectangle of metal plates containing a crystal branch and a blue orb housing the spirit of an ocean world. The artifact acts as a focus that draws magic out quickly and keeps spells ordered and locked onto single targets, allowing for the summoning of wind, rainstorms across entire hemispheres, or even the eruption of vines. After being kept in a sealed crate, the object is eventually seized from its case by the [[Iron King]]. Upon being released, Eurias takes on a gigantic form to engage in combat with the [[Divine Titan]].}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] is a powerful magical focus described as the Life-Giver.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c8">''The Captain'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] is a length of smooth, pale wood with a blue crystal orb on top containing the spirit of an ocean world.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Captain]] intends to use [[Eurias]]' power.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c9">''The Captain'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] is a one-of-a-kind staff that cannot be easily translated, copied, or understood.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c11">''The Captain'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The narrator considers if they will need [[Eurias]] for their next opponent.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c13">''The Captain'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] is [[Varic]]'s staff.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c14">''The Captain'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] can blow wind and rain.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16">''The Captain'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] is kept in a crate with seals.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c30">''The Captain'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] is a place where powers roll through, gushing [[Aether]] and radiating [[life]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c31">''The Captain'', chapter 31</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] is a wand whose power comes from [[World Spirits]] of [[life]] and water.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c33">''The Captain'', chapter 33</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] is used for calling down rainstorms on a whole hemisphere.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c33" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] is a foot-long rectangle of metal plates containing a crystal branch.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c33" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The enchantment of [[Eurias]] is focus.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c33" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] draws magic out faster while keeping the spell ordered.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c33" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The enchantment of [[Eurias]] keeps the spell locked on a single point or target at a time.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c33" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] is an object that [[Benri Vallenar]] might hold.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c34">''The Captain'', chapter 34</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] is a heavy weight in [[Varic Vallenar]]'s mind/storage.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c36">''The Captain'', chapter 36</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] has seen [[Eurias]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c39">''The Captain'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] is a power or entity that can cause vines to erupt and bend the world.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c42">''The Captain'', chapter 42</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] is a character or entity that the narrator did not release.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c42" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] grabs [[Eurias]] from a case.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c44">''The Captain'', chapter 44</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] released [[Eurias]] to take a gigantic form and fight the [[Divine Titan]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c45">''The Captain'', chapter 45</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]] is a staff held by the narrator.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c46">''The Captain'', chapter 46</ref>}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Initially serving as a staff used by the narrator to cast spells, Eurias is eventually revealed to be a destination for travel. This world-level staff, known as the Life-Giver, is crafted from the smooth, pale wood of an [[Aethril]] world-tree and contains a blue crystal orb housing the churning remains of the planet Eurias's own [[World Spirit]]. While the staff runs with [[Aetheric power]] capable of interacting with physical forms, it is a tool for brute magical work that allows the narrator to cast only one spell at a time at a slow speed. During a large-scale spell, the staff provided immense strength and magnified the [[Mirror of Silence]], though the narrator suspected an accidental connection to the entity during a [[Subspace dive]]. Currently held by an [[Archmage]] of the [[Cosmic Path]], the object must be kept in a sealed case to prevent [[Aether]] consumption and possesses the potential to be broken open.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]] is a staff used by the narrator to cast spells.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c9">''The Engineer'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]] is a location the narrator is traveling to.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c28">''The Engineer'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]]'s staff is made from a [[World Spirit]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]] is a world-level staff known as the Life-Giver.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c29">''The Engineer'', chapter 29</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]] is made of smooth, pale wood from an [[Aethril]] world-tree.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c29" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]] is topped with a blue crystal orb containing a churning sea.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c29" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The orb in [[Eurias]] contains the remains of the [[World Spirit]] of the planet [[Eurias]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c29" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]] is a planet.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c29" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]] runs with [[Aetheric power]] that can interact with physical forms.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c29" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]] is a tool or entity used for brute magical work.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36">''The Engineer'', chapter 36</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]] provided the strength for a large-scale spell cast.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]] magnified the [[Mirror of Silence]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c38">''The Engineer'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]] is a power or entity that the narrator can only cast one spell at a time when using.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c38" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The narrator's casting speed through [[Eurias]] was slow.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c38" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]] is a location held by an [[Archmage]] of the [[Cosmic Path]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c46">''The Engineer'', chapter 46</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]] is an object that can be broken open.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47">''The Engineer'', chapter 47</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]] is kept in a case and requires sealing to prevent [[Aether]] consumption.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The narrator thought they might have accidentally connected to [[Eurias]] during the [[Subspace dive]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c54">''The Engineer'', chapter 54</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Eurias is a significant entity to the [[Aether]] that possesses the power to clear out [[Scourge Warrens]]. While casting is notably less potent than if Eurias were performing the magic itself, the entity also functions as a location capable of assisting in travel to another star system. As a blue crystal, it can be liquefied with a touch, allowing it to melt and rise into the narrator's hand. This object is ultimately kept within a crystal case.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Eurias]] is a power or entity that can clear out [[Scourge Warrens]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c4">''The Knight'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Casting is not as powerful as it would have been if [[Eurias]] were casting.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c18">''The Knight'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Eurias]] is a location that can assist in reaching another star system.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c24">''The Knight'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Eurias]] is a crystal that can melt and rise into the narrator's hand.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c36">''The Knight'', chapter 36</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Eurias]] is an object kept in a crystal case.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c38">''The Knight'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Eurias]] is a blue crystal that can be liquefied with a touch.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c40">''The Knight'', chapter 40</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Eurias]] is a significant entity to the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c43">''The Knight'', chapter 43</ref>}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Eurias is a staff crafted from pale wood and topped with a blue orb that houses the [[World Spirit]] of a destroyed ocean planet. This artifact glows with a blue light and possesses the ability to summon miniature rainclouds. Although it was not brought by the narrator's students, the staff carries a metaphysical weight comparable to a planet and is equivalent in magical weight to the [[Voice of Solstice]]. During a ritual, the narrator held the item, which is capable of casting spells from neighboring planets. Ultimately, Eurias was released when the [[Absolute Burial]] spell was crushed, revealing it to be a planet where one can threaten or negotiate with [[World Spirits]] from orbit.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Eurias]] is a staff made of pale wood topped with a blue orb containing the [[World Spirit]] of a destroyed ocean planet.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14">''The Pilot'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Eurias]] is a staff that glows blue and can summon miniature rainclouds.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Eurias]] is a staff that the narrator's students did not bring.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c28">''The Pilot'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Eurias]] has metaphysical weight comparable to a planet.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Eurias]] can cast spells from neighboring planets.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29">''The Pilot'', chapter 29</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Eurias]] is an item held by the narrator during a ritual.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c34">''The Pilot'', chapter 34</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Eurias]] is a being whose magical weight is equivalent to the [[Voice of Solstice]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c39">''The Pilot'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Eurias]] was released by crushing the [[Absolute Burial]] spell.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c44">''The Pilot'', chapter 44</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Eurias]] is a planet where one can negotiate with [[World Spirits]] or threaten them from orbit.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c44" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Artifacts]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon artifacts]]
